Manchester United put Celtic star Kieran Tierney on shortlist of transfer targets

The Old Trafford side have proposed the Hoops star and Fulham teenager Ryan Sessegnon as younger, more economical options in the transfer market.

20:09, 4 JAN 2018Updated21:34, 4 JAN 2018

Kieran Tierney is on Manchester United's shortlist (Image: PA Wire)
Kieran Tierney has been included on a shortlist of left backs Manchester United are considering to strengthen their defence.

The English Premier League club's scouting department has proposed the Celtic star and Fulham teenager Ryan Sessegnon as younger, more economical options for what has been a problem position throughout Jose Mourinho's tenure as Old Trafford manager.

Mourinho is understood to have been impressed with the progress of both Scotland international Tierney, 20, and England youth international Sessegnon, 17. He would, however, prefer to recruit a more experienced left back if the club's budget allows.

As reported by the Daily Record at the beginning of last month, Alex Sandro is Mourinho's preferred choice as a new left back. Juventus, though, have asked for €70million as a transfer fee and have resisted the Brazil international's efforts to be allowed to move to the Premier League.


Kieran Tierney has been on Manchester United's radar for some time (Image: SNS Group)

Sessegnon is another United target (Image: PA Wire)
With every other elite left back tied down to a financially powerful Champions League club, Mourinho also in the market for a central midfielder and a winger, and United working on a limited transfer budget, Tierney and Sessegnon have been proposed as cheaper alternatives.

Tierney is the more experienced option having completed two Champions League campaigns with Celtic and established himself as a full international for Scotland. There is an argument, however, that it would be better for the Scot to transition to Premier League football at a less prominent club – effectively following a path similar to the one that took Andrew Robertson from Dundee United to Liverpool via three campaigns with Hull City.

Tierney's passion for Celtic is also an important factor. The 20-year-old will not push to leave the club he follows and for which his support is so strong he was pictured this week on holiday in Tenerife wearing a centenary shirt.

Kieran Tierney turns up at Celtic pub in Tenerife
Kieran Tierney turns up at Celtic pub in Tenerife (Image: DavidKane/Twitter)
Celtic are also keen to hold on to the player who recently signed a six-year contract extension until 2023.

United's increased interest in Tierney and Sessegnon has also been influenced by a recent improvement in Luke Shaw's attitude and application. The England international has started three consecutive Premier League fixtures as injuries suffered by Antonio Valencia and Matteo Darmian plus Ashley Young's suspension for violent conduct severely limited Mourinho's defensive options.


Kieran Tierney has developed into a star under Brendan Rodgers (Image: Reuters)
It is understood that Mourinho has been impressed by Shaw's efforts to improve his fitness, to fight for a place in the team, and to follow tactical instruction – all issues that bedevilled the inherited £30m signing's first campaign under the Portuguese and led to him starting just one Premier League fixture after October.

Shaw's contract expires in June with United holding an option for a further season. The 22-year-old had been offered to other English clubs, but the improvement in his performances coupled with the cost of recruiting Alex Sandro in the current window has led United to consider an alternative strategy of retaining Shaw while adding a younger deputy such as Tierney or Sessegnon.
